Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Heterologous MYB/bHLH regulator pair component used as a decisive activator to induce high anthocyanin accumulation in tomato fruit when coexpressed with Rosea1.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Driven by the tomato fruit-specific E8 promoter; active in fruit during ripening stages.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation of anthocyanin/phenylpropanoid pathway genes in fruit when coexpressed with Rosea1.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression in tomato (MicroTom) using pDEL.ROS; metabolite profiling by spectrophotometry, HPLC, and LC-MS/MS; expression analysis by suppression subtractive hybridization, northern blot, and microarray.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit-specific transgene expression during ripening in tomato; comparison of independent Del/Ros1 lines C, N, Y, and Z versus wild type.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Anthocyanin biosynthesis within the phenylpropanoid pathway.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Anthocyanin and phenylpropanoid pathway genes; specific targets not stated in the provided text.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Introduced as cDNA in binary vector pDEL.ROS together with Rosea1, each under the E8 promoter. Gene is from snapdragon and acts in tomato as a heterologous regulator.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Tomato PROFOOD oligonucleotide microarray; ArrayExpress array accession A-MEXP-1417 and experiment accession E-MEXP-1830.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Useful for engineering anthocyanin-enriched tomato fruit with enhanced nutritional value and antioxidant capacity.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes expression of anthocyanin/phenylpropanoid-related genes, exact downstream genes not specified in the provided text.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant phytochemical associated with improved nutritional quality; tomato extracts were also evaluated for antioxidant activity and dietary effects in mice.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Delila cDNA was expressed in tomato fruit under the E8 fruit-specific promoter. 2. Together with Rosea1, it caused anthocyanin accumulation in independent transgenic MicroTom fruit lines. 3. The engineered fruit accumulated anthocyanins whose major identified compound was petunidin-3-(p-coumaroylrutinoside)-5-glucoside.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes strong anthocyanin accumulation in tomato fruit when coexpressed with Rosea1.
